content.subtitle: Form
items: subject + have in the correct form + object + past participle

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To indicate that a subject arranges for somebody else to perform a service or task.
The manager **has** her office **redecorated** every year.

content.subtitle: Tense / modal
rows: The manager **has** her office **redecorated** every year.
She **is having** the walls **painted** at the moment.
She **was having** the computers **repaired** when I visited.
She **will have** a new sofa **delivered**. / She **would have** new curtains **put up**.
She **is going to have** a new printer **installed**.
She might stop **having** the office **cleaned** so often.

content.subtitle: Active vs causative
items: Normal active sentence: A decorator paints the office every year.
Causative sentence: The manager **has** the office **painted** every year.

content.subtitle: Have vs get
items: Get can be used instead of have. Get is more informal than have.
I'm going to **have** my laptop repaired. (more formal)
I'm going to **get** my laptop repaired. (more informal)

content.subtitle: By + agent
items: As with the passive, we can use by to show who does the action.
We're having our wedding cake made **by** a local bakery.

content.subtitle: Important rules
items: In the causative, have always comes before the noun and the past participle comes after the noun.
In questions, the past participle stays after the noun: **Did** you **have** the windows **cleaned** yesterday?
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms.

sections.title: A: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

sections.title: B: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

items.sentence: During the class project on The causative, Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: I had my car serviced yesterday.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about The causative, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: Laura is having her hair cut now.

items.sentence: Sara checked the course practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: We will have our wedding photos taken.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about The causative: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: He is having his phone unlocked.

items.sentence: During the school club on The causative, Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: They have their internet installed every year.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about The causative, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: I had my teeth checked this morning.
